issues.tradition_claim: Arjuna's first specific reply after Krishna's rebuke: how can I counter-fight (with arrows) against Bhīṣma and Droṇa, who are worthy of worship? The objection is sincere; the verse marks it as the canonical case of misplaced loyalty as a reason for inaction.
issues.engineering_claim: A senior backend engineer is asked to deprecate an auth service designed nine years ago by the principal engineer. The engineer's objections are couched in respect; none survives technical review. The verse names the trap.
issues.explanation: Re-scoped from battlefield to deprecation. Acceptable translation across domains: the structure (sincere objection grounded in misplaced loyalty) is preserved, the named-figures-as-still-referenced authority is preserved (Bhīṣma still present at all-hands ↔ still appears at all-hands), and the verse's diagnostic move (it names the trap without yet dissolving it) is preserved. The falsifiability explicitly preserves cases where the loyalty IS the right kind (real architectural inquiry, real collaboration needs). No inversion, no contradiction, no weakening of the doctrinal claim. Distortion-test PASS.
